req.title: Reset employee password — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-104 defines four distinct observable outcomes: (1) successful password reset with session invalidation and webhook emission, (2) rejection of an expired token (410), (3) rejection of a policy-violating password (422), and (4) boundary behaviour at the 30-minute expiry window. EP covers the happy-path valid-token partition. BVA targets the 30-minute expiry edge (token at exactly 30 min is still valid; token at 30 min + 1 s is expired). STC models the token state machine: PENDING -> USED (success) and PENDING -> EXPIRED (rejection). NEG covers the invalid-password-policy branch. DT captures the combined condition table (token validity x password policy). Together these five tests satisfy the minimum three required cases (positive, negative, boundary) while achieving multi-dimensional coverage of the requirement.

tests.name: test_reset_password_valid_token_success
preconditions: A valid, non-expired reset token exists for employee test_employee@example.com
The employee has at least one active session
Webhook endpoint is reachable and recorded by <webhook_capture_fixture>
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-compliant new password
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert all prior sessions are invalidated
Assert webhook event 'password.reset' was emitted
expected: HTTP 200 with body confirming password updated
All prior sessions for the employee are invalidated
Webhook event 'password.reset' received by external consumer
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_valid_token_success(valid_reset_token, active_session_ids, webhook_capture_fixture, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "token": valid_reset_token,
        "password": "V@lidP@ssw0rd123!"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"

    # Verify all prior sessions invalidated
    for session_id in active_session_ids:
        session_resp = <check_session_fn>(session_id)
        assert session_resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
            f"Session {session_id} should be invalidated"
        )

    # Verify webhook event emitted
    events = webhook_capture_fixture.get_events(event_type="password.reset")
    assert len(events) >= 1, "Expected at least one password.reset webhook event"
    assert events[-1]["employee_token"] == valid_reset_token or \
           events[-1].get("event") == "password.reset"

tests.name: test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected_410
preconditions: A reset token exists that was issued more than 30 minutes ago (expired)
actions: POST /password/reset with expired token and policy-compliant new password
Assert HTTP 410 response
Assert password was NOT changed
Assert no webhook event was emitted
expected: Response body indicates token has expired
trace: R-WEB-104: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected_410(expired_reset_token, webhook_capture_fixture, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "token": expired_reset_token,
        "password": "V@lidP@ssw0rd123!"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           body.get("code") == "TOKEN_EXPIRED", \
           f"Expected expiry error, got: {body}"

    # No webhook should have fired
    events = webhook_capture_fixture.get_events(event_type="password.reset")
    assert len(events) == 0, f"Unexpected webhook events: {events}"

tests.name: test_reset_password_at_exactly_30_minutes_accepted
preconditions: A reset token exists that was issued exactly 30 minutes ago (at boundary, still valid)
System clock is controlled via <freeze_time_fixture> or token issued_at is set to now()-30min
actions: POST /password/reset with a token aged exactly 30 minutes and a valid password
Assert HTTP 200 — boundary is inclusive (token at 30 min is still valid)
POST /password/reset with a token aged 30 min + 1 second and a valid password
Assert HTTP 410 — one second past boundary is expired
expected: Token at exactly 30 minutes returns HTTP 200
Token at 30 minutes + 1 second returns HTTP 410
trace: R-WEB-104: email link expires after 30 minutes
R-WEB-104: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_at_exactly_30_minutes_accepted(api_base_url, create_token_aged_fn):
    # Token issued exactly 1800 seconds ago — inclusive boundary, still valid
    token_at_boundary = create_token_aged_fn(age_seconds=1800)
    payload = {"token": token_at_boundary, "password": "V@lidP@ssw0rd123!"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Token at 30 min boundary should be accepted, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    # Token issued 1801 seconds ago — one second past boundary, must be expired
    token_past_boundary = create_token_aged_fn(age_seconds=1801)
    payload2 = {"token": token_past_boundary, "password": "V@lidP@ssw0rd123!"}
    resp2 = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload2)
    assert resp2.status_code == 410, (
        f"Token 1 second past 30 min boundary must be rejected, got {resp2.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_reset_password_policy_violation_returns_422
preconditions: A valid, non-expired reset token exists
Password policy requires minimum 12 characters, at least one uppercase, one digit, one special character
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and password 'short' (fails all policy rules)
Assert HTTP 422 with field-level validation error
Assert employee password was NOT changed
Assert no webhook event was emitted
expected: Response body lists password policy violations

tests.name: test_reset_password_token_state_transition_used_then_reuse_rejected
preconditions: A valid, non-expired reset token exists in PENDING state
First use will transition token state to USED/CONSUMED
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and compliant password — first use (PENDING -> USED)
Assert HTTP 200
POST /password/reset with the same token again — second use attempt (USED -> rejected)
Assert HTTP 410 or 400 indicating token already consumed
expected: First request returns HTTP 200 and transitions token to USED state
Second request with same token returns HTTP 410 or 400
Webhook event emitted exactly once (for the first request only)
trace: R-WEB-104: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_token_state_transition_used_then_reuse_rejected(
    valid_reset_token, webhook_capture_fixture, api_base_url
):
    payload = {"token": valid_reset_token, "password": "V@lidP@ssw0rd123!"}

    # First use: PENDING -> USED
    resp1 = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200, f"First use expected 200, got {resp1.status_code}"

    events_after_first = webhook_capture_fixture.get_events(event_type="password.reset")
    assert len(events_after_first) == 1, "Expected exactly 1 webhook event after first use"

    # Second use: USED -> rejected
    payload2 = {"token": valid_reset_token, "password": "An0therV@lid#99"}
    resp2 = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/password/reset", json=payload2)
    assert resp2.status_code in (400, 410), (
        f"Reuse of consumed token expected 400 or 410, got {resp2.status_code}"
    )

    events_after_second = webhook_capture_fixture.get_events(event_type="password.reset")
    assert len(events_after_second) == 1, "Webhook must not fire on rejected reuse"
